learned: SPIKE 3 — ToS/licensing per market-page source. (1) KAGGLE: kaggle.com Terms of Use (effective June 22, 2025) sec 12 prohibits any crawling/scraping ("'Crawls,' 'scrapes,' or 'spiders' any page, data, or portion of or relating to the Services or Content (through use of manual or automated means)") and "Copies or stores any significant portion of the Content"; sec 6 limits use to "your own internal, personal, non-commercial use, and not on behalf of or for the benefit of any third party." So mirroring by scraping kaggle.com = NO. HOWEVER the official Meta Kaggle dataset (kaggle/meta-kaggle) is licensed Apache 2.0 (license changed from CC-BY-NC-SA to Apache 2.0 in Aug 2023, stated on the dataset page), updated daily, and contains Competitions.csv, Submissions.csv (public scores), Episodes/EpisodeAgents (agent-ladder rankings), Datasets.csv (dataset metadata) — mirror standings + republish dataset metadata VIA META KAGGLE = YES with Apache-2.0 attribution. Deep-linking = YES (no anti-linking clause). Verdict: MIRROR-via-Meta-Kaggle; never scrape the site. (2) ARC-AGI-3 / arcprize.org: site ToS sec 2 — "no part of the Services and no Content or Marks may be copied, reproduced, aggregated, republished... without our express prior written permission"; sec 4 prohibits scraping AND "unauthorized framing of or linking to the Services" (boilerplate but present); license granted is personal, non-commercial. Scorecard norms (docs.arcprize.org/scorecards): "Scorecards are not public, however you can share replays from scorecards created via the API with others. Local scorecards cannot be shared." Scorecard URLs exist (arcprize.org/scorecards/{id}) but are not public. ARC-AGI-2 task data repo = Apache-2.0; ARC-AGI-3-Agents repo = MIT; ARC-AGI-3 games are API-served with no open data license found. Verdict: LINK-ONLY for leaderboard standings (mirroring needs written permission); plain deep-links only, no framing/embedding; user-consented replay share-links are the sanctioned sharing surface. (3) POKEAGENT CHALLENGE: pokeagentchallenge.com and pokeagent.github.io have NO terms of service, privacy policy, or license anywhere — footer is only "© 2025 PokéAgent Challenge. All rights reserved." plus contact email/Discord; only legal-adjacent text is a NeurIPS 2025 BibTeX citation. Academic competition (Princeton/UT Austin/NYU/JHU/GDM/CMU, leads Seth Karten & Jake Grigsby); live ladder at battling.pokeagentchallenge.com/ladder (Pokémon Showdown server). Verdict: UNCLEAR — terms gap confirmed and flagged per spike instruction; default copyright applies; deep-link freely, mirror only after asking organizers (low-friction: email/Discord); the Showdown-API programmatic path suggests tolerance but is legally unverified. (4) SCALE SEAL (labs.scale.com/leaderboard; scale.com/leaderboard 308-redirects there): Scale Website T&C sec 1 licenses access "solely with supported browsers through the Internet for your own internal purposes"; prohibits "modify or make derivative works based upon the Sites", "copy any features, functions, or graphics", and access "to (a) build a competitive product or service"; no anti-linking clause; Scale owns all site IP. Leaderboard page offers no API/data download and no citation/attribution guidance ("contact leaderboards@scale.com" to be evaluated). SWE-Bench Pro public+private standings confirmed hosted there. Verdict: LINK-ONLY (raw scores are arguably uncopyrightable facts, but the access-contract restriction stands; safe default is deep-link to labs.scale.com/leaderboard/swe_bench_pro_public). (5) HUGGINGFACE DATASETS: ScaleAI/SWE-bench_Pro has NO license anywhere — HF API cardData.license=None, no license: tag, zero license mentions in the dataset-card README (verified by grep of raw README). Companion GitHub repo scaleapi/SWE-bench_Pro-os is MIT (harness code only). Per the SWE-Bench Pro paper (arXiv:2509.16941), public-set instances are deliberately sourced from strong-copyleft (GPL) repos as a contamination deterrent — so instance contents carry upstream GPL obligations even though the dataset itself grants nothing. Verdict: republishing factual metadata (name/description/row counts/link) = OK as facts + hub norms; mirroring dataset CONTENTS = UNCLEAR/NO (no license grant). Terminal-Bench: GitHub laude-institute/terminal-bench = Apache-2.0; HF zai-org/terminal-bench-2-verified cardData.license = apache-2.0; laude-institute/sandboxes-tasks = apache-2.0. Verdict: MIRROR OK (metadata and data) with Apache-2.0 attribution + license notice. (6) WECO (terms at weco.ai/terms-of-use — note /terms is 404): Ownership of Content clause — "you (a) retain your ownership rights in Input and (b) own the Output. We hereby assign to you all our right, title, and interest... in and to Output." So a user's own exported run/lineage JSON is user-owned Output → third-party display on agentdex WITH the user's consent = YES. But "What You Cannot Do" prohibits "Automatically or programmatically extracting data or Output" — agentdex must NOT scrape/pull from Weco itself; the user must export and bring the JSON (BYO-export matches the M2 BYO-creds architecture). Privacy policy grants GDPR-style portability ("right to receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format"), reinforcing the user-export path. Share-link embedding: no public share-link feature is documented anywhere (docs only say dashboard lets you "share results with your team"); terms are silent on embedding → UNCLEAR, treat as unavailable until Weco confirms. Caveat: the Terms self-scope to individual/consumer, personal non-commercial use ("AIDE for individuals"), so commercial/enterprise use may sit under a separate unpublished agreement — flag for counsel if agentdex monetizes the integration. OVERALL: default link-out holds everywhere except the three green lanes — Kaggle-via-Meta-Kaggle (Apache 2.0), Terminal-Bench (Apache-2.0), and user-consented Weco Output display; PokeAgent needs an organizer ask; ARC-AGI-3 and SEAL stay strictly link-only.

evidence.cite: https://www.kaggle.com/terms (Terms of Use, effective June 22, 2025), sec 12
evidence.note: Prohibits "'Crawls,' 'scrapes,' or 'spiders' any page, data, or portion of or relating to the Services or Content (through use of manual or automated means)" and "Copies or stores any significant portion of the Content" — bars scraping kaggle.com leaderboards directly. Sec 6: "your own internal, personal, non-commercial use, and not on behalf of or for the benefit of any third party." No anti-linking clause anywhere in the ToU.

License: Apache 2.0 (links to apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0); page states "We also updated the license on Meta Kaggle from CC-BY-NC-SA to Apache 2.0" (Aug 2023). Tables include Competitions.csv, Submissions.csv, Episodes/EpisodeAgents.csv, Datasets.csv, updated daily — the sanctioned route to mirror Kaggle standings and dataset metadata.

Sec 2: "no part of the Services and no Content or Marks may be copied, reproduced, aggregated, republished... without our express prior written permission"; personal non-commercial license only. Sec 4 prohibits scraping/data-mining tools AND "unauthorized framing of or linking to the Services" — leaderboard mirroring requires written permission; keep to plain deep-links.

Exact sharing norm: "Scorecards are not public, however you can share replays from scorecards created via the API with others. Local scorecards cannot be shared." Scorecard URLs follow arcprize.org/scorecards/{scorecard_id}. GitHub API: arcprize/ARC-AGI-2 = Apache-2.0, arcprize/ARC-AGI-3-Agents = MIT; no open license found for ARC-AGI-3 game/leaderboard content.

NO terms of service, privacy policy, or license exists on either site — footer carries only "© 2025 PokéAgent Challenge. All rights reserved.", contact email/Discord, and a NeurIPS 2025 BibTeX. Ladder at battling.pokeagentchallenge.com/ladder. Gap confirmed per spike instruction; mirroring rights unverifiable without asking organizers.

Access licensed "solely with supported browsers through the Internet for your own internal purposes"; may not "modify or make derivative works based upon the Sites", "copy any features, functions, or graphics", or access the Sites to "build a competitive product or service". No anti-linking clause. labs.scale.com/leaderboard (scale.com/leaderboard 308-redirects there) hosts SWE-Bench Pro public+private standings, offers no API/download, no citation guidance.

cardData.license = None, no license: tag, zero license/copyright/GPL mentions in the dataset card — the dataset ships with NO license grant. GitHub scaleapi/SWE-bench_Pro-os = MIT (harness code only). Paper arXiv:2509.16941: public set deliberately built from strong-copyleft (GPL) repos as a contamination deterrent, so instance contents carry upstream GPL terms.

Terminal-Bench licensing is clean: HF zai-org/terminal-bench-2-verified cardData.license = apache-2.0; GitHub laude-institute/terminal-bench = Apache-2.0; HF laude-institute/sandboxes-tasks = apache-2.0. Mirror freely with attribution + license notice.

evidence.cite: https://www.weco.ai/terms-of-use (Ownership of Content; What You Cannot Do)
evidence.note: "you (a) retain your ownership rights in Input and (b) own the Output. We hereby assign to you all our right, title, and interest... in and to Output" → user-consented third-party display of exported run/lineage JSON is permitted. But users may not go "Automatically or programmatically extracting data or Output" → agentdex must never pull from Weco itself; user-export only. Terms self-scope to individual/consumer non-commercial use ("AIDE for individuals") — enterprise scope unverified. No public share-link/embed feature documented (docs only mention team sharing via dashboard); weco.ai/terms is 404, canonical URL is /terms-of-use.

next: Market page implementation: build the Kaggle lane on the Meta Kaggle dataset (Apache 2.0) via the official Kaggle datasets API download — Competitions.csv + Submissions.csv + EpisodeAgents.csv — with Apache-2.0 attribution; never scrape kaggle.com HTML.

Ship ARC-AGI-3 and Scale SEAL / SWE-Bench Pro standings as LINK-OUT cards only (plain anchor links, no iframes/framing, no cached score tables); for ARC, surface user-consented replay share-links as the interactive element since replays are the explicitly sanctioned shareable artifact.

Email/Discord the PokeAgent organizers (contacts in pokeagentchallenge.com footer; leads Seth Karten, Jake Grigsby) asking written OK to mirror ladder standings via the Showdown API; until answered, link-out to battling.pokeagentchallenge.com/ladder and record the terms gap in the market-page provenance notes.

Terminal-Bench lane may fully mirror metadata + standings-relevant data under Apache-2.0 (cite laude-institute/terminal-bench and zai-org/terminal-bench-2-verified with license notice); SWE-bench_Pro HF card gets facts-only metadata (name, counts, link) — do not redistribute instance contents (no license grant + upstream GPL).

Weco integration must be user-export-push only (user uploads their own run/lineage JSON they own per the Output-assignment clause); do not build any agentdex-side fetcher against Weco dashboards/API surfaces (programmatic extraction prohibited), and treat share-link embedding as unavailable until Weco confirms a public share feature; flag the consumer-scope caveat to counsel if the integration is monetized.

Optional hardening: ask Scale (leaderboards@scale.com) and ARC Prize for written permission if the product later wants cached SEAL/ARC standings; both ToS make written permission the only mirror path.
key_facts: Kaggle ToU (2025-06-22) bans all scraping and bulk copying of site Content, but the official Meta Kaggle dataset is Apache 2.0 (relicensed from CC-BY-NC-SA in Aug 2023) and contains competitions, submission scores, episode-agent rankings, and dataset metadata updated daily — mirror via Meta Kaggle, deep-link freely.

arcprize.org ToS requires express prior written permission to republish any Content and prohibits scraping and 'unauthorized framing of or linking'; ARC-AGI-3 scorecards are explicitly NOT public — only replays from API-created scorecards are shareable ('Local scorecards cannot be shared').

PokeAgent Challenge has zero legal terms on both pokeagentchallenge.com and pokeagent.github.io (only an all-rights-reserved footer + BibTeX) — the terms gap the spike anticipated is confirmed; mirroring rights unverifiable without contacting organizers.

Scale's website T&C limits access to internal purposes, bans derivative works/copying and building competitive products from the Sites; SEAL leaderboard (labs.scale.com/leaderboard, hosting SWE-Bench Pro public+private standings) has no data API and no citation guidance → link-only.

HF ScaleAI/SWE-bench_Pro dataset has NO license field or license text at all (verified via HF API + raw README grep); its public instances are intentionally sourced from strong-copyleft GPL repos (arXiv:2509.16941), so contents redistribution is unclear-to-prohibited; terminal-bench is cleanly Apache-2.0 across GitHub and HF mirrors.

Weco assigns all right/title/interest in Output to the user (user-owned run/lineage JSON → consented third-party display OK) but prohibits programmatic extraction of data/Output, has no documented public share-link feature, and its published terms cover only individual non-commercial use — canonical terms URL is weco.ai/terms-of-use (/terms is 404).
Default recommendation holds: link-out everywhere except three green lanes — Kaggle-via-Meta-Kaggle (Apache 2.0), Terminal-Bench (Apache-2.0), user-consented Weco Output display.
